frameworks.name: decoded neurofeedback (DecNef)
frameworks.one_line: rt-fMRI multi-voxel pattern manipulation drives targeted neural state shifts

frameworks.name: multi-voxel pattern decoding
frameworks.one_line: voxel-pattern decoder used as an online feedback signal to participants

frameworks.name: nonlinear mathematical modeling of learning
frameworks.one_line: parametrizes magnitude, directional asymmetry, retention, and interference of DecNef

core_question: Can the same participants learn to induce opposing neural patterns and bidirectional changes in perceptual confidence across two DecNef sessions?
key_assumptions: fine-grained voxel patterns can be modulated by reinforcement-based neurofeedback
perceptual confidence is supported by decodable voxel-pattern states
manipulations: direction of DecNef training (up- vs down-regulation of confidence)
order of the two directional sessions (counterbalanced)
key_findings: DecNef induced bidirectional confidence changes within the same participants
up-regulation effects were more prominent than down-regulation
induced changes persisted after a week-long interval
second-session effects were markedly diminished, indicating anterograde learning interference
interpretations: decoded neurofeedback can causally and bidirectionally shift a metacognitive variable within an individual
training order and directionality interact, with strong carry-over interference across sessions
